instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on How to Buy Critical Illness Cover with a Life Insurance Policy - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: critical illness cover, life insurance policy, health insurance policy, critical illness plan
Body Content:
When you are thinking of buying certain financial products, insurance should have a place at the top somewhere. The sheer rising cost of healthcare, especially when it comes to critical illness, should compel you to buy insurance. Inflation affects the gamut of products and services across industries, and healthcare costs increase too. Therefore, individuals must plan their finances in such a way that there is always an adequate amount in their kitty for critical illness cover.  You can easily purchase cover for critical illness with life insurance as well as health insurance plans.
What is a critical illness cover?
Critical illness cover is coverage that provides the insured with a benefit in a lump sum. This pays for the cost of treatment and care that goes towards particular illnesses and recovery expenses. Furthermore, if you have a surplus amount left over, you are able to use it to settle debt such as loans taken through equated monthly instalments (EMIs).
Once the insurer makes a payment to the insured, a critical illness covers lapses. Generally, any amount in a lump sum is paid to the holder of a life insurance policy, or a health insurance policy, after a survival period of a month of contracting any illness. Moreover, you are permitted to purchase more than a single critical illness cover. Each of the policies you purchase will pay for any critical illness. While you may have decided to have some critical illness cover, it is important to know how this kind of cover is included with a health insurance policy, and a life insurance policy.
How Critical Illness Cover Works in the Case of Life Insurance
When you buy a life insurance policy, you can easily purchase critical illness cover with it. This can be done as a rider, or as a cover which is a standalone policy in itself. Let’s look at each of these:
Critical Illness Riders - In terms of critical illness riders, there are two kinds in which premiums change according to the riders. Here are the two kinds explained in more detail:
Additional Benefit Rider - The sum assured for critical illness cover is added to your existing policy as an add-on cover. This means that the sum assured for critical illness will be added to the base sum of your existing policy. In the case of this kind of policy, your life insurance policy can continue with the same assured sum even though the rider for critical illness applies. For instance, let’s assume you have purchased a life insurance policy amounting to Rs. 1 crore along with an add-on critical illness rider amounting to Rs. 40 lakhs. Now, in case you raise a claim for a payout worth Rs. 40 lakhs in your critical illness cover, your base amount of Rs. 1 crore remains unaffected and is not reduced. In such a life insurance policy, the premium on the Rs. 1 crore amount is higher, relative to choosing a critical illness cover rider as an accelerated benefit.
Accelerated Benefit Rider - In this case, the sum assured for critical illness is reduced from your policy assured sum and is paid out once a claim is raised. The life insurance policy then continues to hold good with the sum that is reduced. For instance, let us assume that you have a life insurance policy of Rs. 1 crore, with a division such that Rs. 60 lakh is your main policy cover and Rs. 40 lakh is your accelerated cover for critical illness. If you raise a claim for Rs. 40 lakhs, the policy continues to operate with a reduced cover (of Rs. 60 lakh). The premium in such a situation will be lower. In the event of your unfortunate demise without raising any critical illness claim, the insurer will pay your nominee the total Rs. 1 crore amount.
Standalone Riders - With such critical illness cover, the term insurance policy will give your family financial security in the event of an unfortunate death but will give you financial security against critical illness care. These policies cover fifty life-threatening diseases like kidney failure, cancer, cardiac disease, etc.
How Critical Illness Cover Works in the Case of Health Insurance
In a regular health insurance policy, you get hospitalisation cover as part of your plan. However, critical illness covers as defined in a health plan gives a lump sum benefit in case the policyholder is diagnosed with any critical illness that the policy covers. In terms of health insurance policies, critical illness plans are designed to offer coverage for costs overrun (because of high indemnity cover and the considerable treatment cost), and additionally consider changes in lifestyle as a result of illness, leading to lower earning capacity of the insured.
Conclusion
A critical illness plan is essential these days, given the rise in the incidence of critical illnesses and the increasing cost involved in their treatment. Experts suggest that a standalone critical illness policy is a must-have product, along with a health insurance or life insurance policy. This ensures a financial safeguard for families whose incomes may be otherwise eroded due to enormous medical expenses.

output.final_text:
How to Buy Critical Illness Cover with a Life Insurance Policy Keywords: critical illness cover, life insurance policy, health insurance policy, critical illness plan When you are thinking of buying certain financial products, insurance can be considered as one of the options. The sheer rising cost of healthcare, especially when it comes to critical illness, should compel you to buy insurance. Inflation affects a wide range of products and services across industries, including healthcare expenses. Therefore, individuals must plan their finances in such a way that there is always an adequate amount in their kitty for critical illness cover. You can easily purchase cover for critical illness with life insurance as well as health insurance plans. What is a critical illness cover? Critical illness cover is coverage that provides the insured with a benefit in a lump sum on getting diagnosed with a critical illness covered under the policy, on payment of due premiums. This pays for the cost of treatment and care that goes towards particular illnesses and recovery expenses. Furthermore, if you have a surplus amount left over, you can use it to settle any loans) or meet other expenses. Once the insurer makes a payment to the insured, the critical illness covers expires. Generally, any amount in a lump sum is paid to the holder of a life insurance policy, after a survival period of a month of contracting any illness. This may vary depending on the product terms and conditions. Moreover, you are permitted to purchase more than a single critical illness cover. How Critical Illness Cover Works in the Case of Life Insurance When you buy a life insurance policy, you can easily purchase critical illness cover with it by paying nominal extra premium. This can be done as a rider, or as a cover which is a standalone policy in itself. Let’s look at each of these:
● Critical Illness Riders - In terms of critical illness riders, there are two kinds :
1. Additional Benefit Rider - Critical Illness Rider is a common term insurance rider that can
assist a person in ensuring the financial stability of their family during difficult times. A critical illness rider is an optional coverage that may be added to a base insurance policy for an additional premium. A lump sum amount is granted to the life assured's family under the critical illness rider if the life assured is diagnosed with a designated critical illness during the policy duration as per the policy terms and conditions. Critical illness riders insure against significant life-threatening diseases that are covered under the rider. This rider gives financial assistance to the family of the life assured at tough times and assists them in meeting the treatment costs of the diagnosed illness. .For instance, let’s assume you have purchased a life insurance policy amounting to Rs. 1 crore along with an add-on critical illness rider amounting to Rs. 40 lakhs. Now, in case you raise a claim for a payout worth Rs.
40 lakhs in your critical illness cover, your base amount of Rs. 1 crore remains unaffected and is not reduced. 2. Accelerated Benefit Rider - In this case, the sum assured for critical illness is reduced from
your policy assured sum and is paid out once a claim is raised. The life insurance policy then continues to hold good with the sum that is reduced. For instance, let us assume that you have a life insurance policy of Rs. 1 crore, with a division such that Rs. 60 lakh is your main policy cover and Rs. 40 lakh is your accelerated cover for critical illness. If you raise a claim for Rs. 40 lakhs, the policy continues to operate with a reduced cover (of Rs. 60 lakh). The premium in such a situation will be lower. In the event of your unfortunate demise without raising any critical illness claim, the insurer will pay your nominee the total Rs. 1 crore amount depending on the policy terms and conditions.
● Standalone plans - Standalone health insurance plans provide more coverage and charge greater prices. Riders with life insurance are less expensive. If you are looking for coverage for Personal Accident and Critical Illness, you have two options. This coverage is available as a rider on your life insurance policy or as a separate policy from health insurance providers. Conclusion A critical illness plan is essential these days, given the rise in the incidence of critical illnesses and the increasing cost involved in their treatment. This ensures a financial safeguard for families whose incomes may be otherwise eroded due to enormous medical expenses.
